commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` so prefixed hook session-key overrides do not warn and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +15. Total +15 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` still fires solely on `allowRequestSessionKey`, even when `allowedSessionKeyPrefixes` is configured.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated follow-up can fix the docs mismatch without changing the PR's runtime or config behavior.

target.title: fix #17447: [Bug]: Security audit: allow suppressing acknowledged findings + fix false positives
target.body:
## Summary

- Partially addresses #17447 by removing the false positive hook audit warning when request-supplied session keys are constrained by `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`.
- Keeps the warning when `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey=true` is enabled without prefixes, where callers can still choose arbitrary session key shapes.
- Adds a focused regression test for the prefixed request-session-key audit configuration.
- Why it matters / User impact: Operators can enable request-scoped hook session keys with an explicit prefix allowlist without the security audit claiming an arbitrary-session-key risk that the configured prefix boundary already prevents.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause for issue #17447, not a fan-out or merged-PR follow-up.

## Root Cause

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for a security audit predicate false positive.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the diff is limited to the hook audit predicate and its focused regression coverage, and the source CLI audit path was exercised with isolated home, config, and state.
- Root cause: `collectHooksHardeningFindings` already normalized `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`, and the missing-prefix finding used that normalized contract, but the older `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` branch ignored the normalized prefix invariant and fired solely on `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey=true`, causing prefixed configurations to be reported as arbitrary session-key override risk.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The fix changes the source audit invariant before findings are emitted: `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` now uses the same `allowRequestSessionKey && allowedPrefixes.length === 0` condition as the missing-prefix finding, so only configurations that permit arbitrary request session-key shapes produce the warning instead of masking the symptom after report generation.

## Scope

- What did NOT change: This PR does not change request routing, hook authorization, `security.audit.suppressions`, the small-model heuristic, reverse-proxy trusted-header findings, config defaults, migrations, or the public config schema. The related open PR / same-contract scan found no linked open PR for #17447 and no public-config competition PR in the evidence directory.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The source-of-truth is the hook hardening audit collector that turns normalized config into security findings. This patch changes that collector predicate directly, not a report post-processor or display mirror, and it preserves backward compatibility/default behavior because unprefixed request session-key overrides continue to emit both warnings.
- Patch quality notes: Patch-quality warnings considered: public-config surface, security-boundary, and session-state. The change is not symptom masking; it narrows one finding to the same source invariant already used by the stricter missing-prefix finding, with a two-file diff and focused regression coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw security audit --json` no longer emits `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` or `hooks.request_session_key_prefixes_missing` for a hooks config that enables request session keys but constrains them with `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`.
- Real environment tested: Linux source checkout, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under the evidence directory.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
HOME=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/audit-home \
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/audit-home/.openclaw \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/hooks-prefix-audit-config.json \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/audit-state \
pnpm --dir /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-17447 exec node --import tsx -e 'import { runCli } from "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-17447/src/cli/run-main.ts"; await runCli(["node","openclaw","security","audit","--json"]);' \
> /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/hooks-prefix-source-cli-audit-output.json

node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const report=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync("/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-17447-evidence/hooks-prefix-source-cli-audit-output.json","utf8")); const ids=new Set((report.findings||[]).map(f=>f.checkId)); for (const id of ["hooks.request_session_key_enabled","hooks.request_session_key_prefixes_missing"]) { if (ids.has(id)) { console.error(`unexpected finding: ${id}`); process.exit(1); } } console.log(`isolated source cli audit proof ok: ${report.findings.length} findings, hook sessionKey findings absent`);'
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
isolated source cli audit proof ok: 9 findings, hook sessionKey findings absent
```
- Observed result after fix: The actual source CLI `security audit --json` path completed and the parsed JSON report did not include either hook session-key finding for the prefixed configuration.
- What was not tested: Built-package `openclaw.mjs` was not exercised because this source worktree does not include `dist/entry.mjs`; the source CLI command path was exercised instead. I did not test or change the other #17447 topics: general audit suppressions, small-model sandboxing heuristics, or reverse-proxy trusted-header warnings.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: The regression test constructs a hooks config with `allowRequestSessionKey: true` and `allowedSessionKeyPrefixes: ["hook:"]`, then verifies that both hook session-key findings are absent while the existing unprefixed warning coverage remains intact.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test locks the audit collector behavior at the same source-of-truth predicate changed by this patch, while the source CLI proof separately exercises the real `security audit --json` command path.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: merge risk: security-boundary, session-state, public_config.
- Risk explanation: The touched code reports security audit findings and mentions session keys, so the risk is reviewer concern about weakening a security boundary or changing public config semantics. The patch does not relax hook authorization or routing; it only stops claiming arbitrary session-key shape risk when a configured prefix allowlist already constrains the accepted key space.
- Why acceptable: The compatibility/default behavior is unchanged for unprefixed configurations, which continue to emit both warnings. Prefixed configurations now align both findings to the same normalized-prefix contract, and the out of scope areas are explicitly left unchanged.

## Additional checks

```text
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
passed with no output

pnpm --dir /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-17447 exec vitest run src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       14 passed (14)
```
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
